content.content:
In script.sh line 3:
if [[ $1 == "" ]]
     ^-- SC2236: Use -n instead of ! -z.

In script.sh line 7:
echo $var
     ^-- SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting.

In script.sh line 12:
cat $file
    ^-- SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting.

In script.sh line 20:
rm -rf $dir
       ^-- SC2115: Use "${var:?}" to ensure this never expands to /*.

In script.sh line 25:
[ $x == $y ]
  ^-- SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing.
  ^-- SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing.

In script.sh line 30:
grep foo /var/log/*.log
           ^-- SC2231: Quote to prevent word expansion.

In script.sh line 33:
IFS=""
^-- SC2034: IFS appears unused. Verify use (or export if used externally).

In script.sh line 40:
cd /some/dir
echo "Now in $(pwd)"
^-- SC2164: Use cd ... || exit in case cd fails.

In script.sh line 45:
for f in $(ls *.txt)
         ^-- SC2045: Iterating over ls output is fragile. Use globs.

In script.sh line 50:
while read line; do echo $line; done < file
                        ^-- SC2086: Double quote to prevent globbing.
